cards.back: A network of computers that work together to validate and record transactions without a central authority
A process by which nodes on a network agree on the state of the blockchain, ensuring the integrity of the data
A self-executing contract with the terms of the agreement written directly into lines of code
A one-way mathematical function that takes input data and produces a fixed-length string of characters, often used in blockchain verification
A reward given to miners for validating transactions and adding new blocks to the blockchain
